entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
Discover mathematical concepts using manipulatives. Learn strategies and build agility through mental math. RightStart C Primary Skill Focus: Addition, Subtraction, Place Value

This topic is included in the following course(s): Mathematics: Grade 3

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content:
Students who are new to RightStart Math should take the placement test on the RightStart website (rightstartmath.com). 
Prerequisite: RightStart B or appropriate skill proficiency

rows.label: Math Lessons: Grade 3
rows.books: RightStart Mathematics Level C 2nd Ed. Book Bundle

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Math Lessons: Grade 3
☐ Read RightStart Level C Teacher's Guide p.ii-v.

☐ Organize manipulatives and supplies for easy access.

☐ Decide when in the day you will play math games and how you plan to keep up with assigning them.

☐ If you downloaded the RightStart ebook, print out enough copies of each page and put them in a binder for easy access.

books.title: RightStart Mathematics Level C 2nd Ed. Book Bundle

blocks.text: The Alveary team strongly recommends RightStart Math because we know that it is comprehensive, uses methods of inquiry and discovery, has built-in mental math practice, and emphasizes ideas (through manipulatives) before algorithms. They also support teachers with helpful webinars and great customer service. However, resist the temptation to hop from one curriculum to another. Find a program that works for your students and stick with it. Lessons do not always follow the exact same arc, but below are a few tips to keep in mind.

Your lessons will go more smoothly if you work through them as a student before trying to teach them. Try to stay several lessons ahead of your student so that you can see where the program is going. Note the places where you have to stop and think, because those are probably going to be places where students are going to be challenged as well.
Resist the temptation to fall back on just teaching a memorizable procedure when the student struggles with a concept. Growth happens in the struggle. Work on resilience by modeling a growth mindset.

When your student hits a wall during a lesson, stop and move on to the next subject in your day or take a break. Explain that even when you stop working on a problem, your brain continues to work on it while you do other things, and you don’t even know it. Many times, when you come back to the problem the next day, it comes more easily.

It is critically important to keep up the recommended pace for math study, even in the early grades, because the cumulative effects of slower pacing can have real consequences in terms of high school graduation and college entrance. Math is one subject in which you really cannot afford to get behind, even if that means you need to continue mathematics study between terms. We are referring here to the consistent daily completion of lessons and not the pace of your student’s understanding. The pace of understanding will vary based on many factors. You will need to move at your students’ pace to make sure they are mastering the material. Not all students are expected to complete calculus in high school.

Look for ways to bring math into everyday life and conversation "by the way" - through cooking, gardening, house projects, health, games, etc. Students should discover how math is useful and beautiful, and how it helps us explain and navigate the world.

terms.content:
· RightStart Math Level C: Make a 3 x 5 array with centimeter cubes. How much is it? What is the perimeter? What is the area?

· RightStart Math Level C: Write 4178 in words. Show it on AL Abacus. Build it with place value cards.

· RightStart Math Level C: Solve: 
2453 + 5197 = 
49 + 27 + 1 =
Use >, <, or = to make this true:
3 x 2 ____ 3 + 2

· RightStart Math Level C: Solve. First, show your work using a Part-Part-Whole Circle Set. Then write the equation. 
While traveling on vacation, the Smith family drove 2,546 km. The Jones family drove 1,942 km. How much farther did the Smith family drive?

· RightStart Math Level C: Use >, <, or = to make each true: 
3694 ____ 3964 
399 + 2 ____ 401 
7832 + 495 ____ 7832 - 495 
Solve:
6427 - 3675 =

· RightStart Math Level C: Show the following on your clock: 
6:09 
10:45
